• 3D racing game with voxel terrain generation using Three.js
  • Real-time multiplayer using MQTT protocol
  • Dynamic terrain with multiple biomes (grass, desert, snow, mars, caves)
  • Interactive UI overlay with minimap, speedometer, chat, and soundboard
  • Mobile controls and responsive design
    const TerrainSystem = class { constructor(scene, game, renderDist) { this.scene = scene; this.game = game; this.settings = { seed: 12345, step: 0.05, unit: 50, water: 0, timex: 0, timez: 0, biome: 'grass' }; this.noise = new SimplexNoise(this.settings.seed); this.chunks = {}; this.chunkSize = 100; this.resolution = 32; this.activeRadius = renderDist || 3; } }
    const Car = class { constructor(scene, color, isLocal = false) { this.scene = scene; this.isLocal = isLocal; this.mesh = this.createMesh(color); this.scene.add(this.mesh); this.pos = new THREE.Vector3(0, 100, 0); this.vel = new THREE.Vector3(0, 0, 0); this.vy = 0; this.speed = 0; this.nitro = 100; this.score = 0; this.onGround = false; } }

This voxel racing game features procedurally generated terrain with multiple biomes, real-time multiplayer networking via MQTT, and a comprehensive UI system. The game includes physics-based car movement, dynamic lighting cycles, particle effects, and supports both desktop and mobile controls. Players can customize their vehicles, chat with others, and experience different terrain types including caves with ceilings and various environmental challenges.
